name: Albedo Clarity

overview.value: Albedo Space is an American aerospace manufacturing company founded in 2020 that develops and manufactures Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) satellites. It launched its first satellite, Clarity-1, in March 2025 aboard SpaceX's Transporter-13 rideshare mission, operating at an altitude of 320 km with a 10 cm panchromatic camera, 40 cm near-infrared multispectral band, and a 2 m thermal infrared camera. Clarity-1 lost communications after about nine months and reentered Earth's atmosphere on 10 June 2026. In October 2025 Albedo announced a strategic pivot from selling commercial satellite imagery to developing VLEO satellite buses for other payload operators.

base.reason: eoPortal mission page (ESA-run aggregator); page states the optical imager has a 'ground sample distance (GSD) of 10 cm at nadir' (thermal imager 2 m); registry v2 EO spec backfill, cite-facts-only per terms

revisit.value: 1.5 revisits per day, increasing to 3-5 revisits per day (full constellation)

base.reason: eoPortal mission page; page states the full constellation will provide 'a global revisit rate of 1.5 revisits per day, increasing to 3-5 revisits per day'

eoPortal mission page; Table 1 states the optical imager captures Panchromatic 400-700 nm, Blue 450-510 nm, Green 510-580 nm, Red 630-690 nm, Near-Infrared 770-895 nm, and the payload adds a thermal infrared (LWIR) imager

notes: Added in the audit Phase 3 backfill (2026-07-08), verify-then-add. eoPortal EO spec backfill (2026-07-09): Clarity constellation is in development (Clarity-1 launched March 2025), so the stated figures are design specifications; the optical GSD is 10 cm at nadir, multispectral 40 cm, thermal 2 m. Swath width not stated on the page, left null. No page 'last updated' date was shown.
